title: Cortex XDR device control violations
content:
Queries Cortex XDR for device control violations for the specified hosts, IP address, or XDR endpoint ID. It then communicates via email with the involved users to understand the nature of the incident and if the user connected the device. 
All the collected data will be displayed in the XDR device control incident layout.
This playbook can also be associated with Cortex XDR device control violation job to periodically query and investigate XDR device control violations. In this configuration, the playbook will only communicate with the involved users.

Cortex XSOAR/XSIAM SOAR playbook from the Cortex XSOAR pack (CortexXDR, xsoar-supported).

Use cases: Malware.

Inputs — TimeStamp: Timestamp in relative date format for query device control events
from Cortex XDR.
For example "1 day", "3 weeks".; EndpointID: Comma-separated list of endpoint IDs to filter results for.; Hostname: Comma-separated list of hostnames to filter results for.; IPAddress: Comma-separated list of endpoints IP Addresses to filter results for.; MessageSubject: The subject of the message for communication with the involved users.; MessageBody: A body of the message for communication with the involved users..

Integrations/scripts used: IsIntegrationAvailable, Set, SetGridField, ad-get-user, xdr-get-endpoint-device-control-violations.

In this configuration, the playbook will only communicate with the involved users."

  playbookInputQuery:
- key: MessageSubject

- key: MessageBody

  required: false
  description: A body of the message for communication with the involved users.
  playbookInputQuery:
